title: Chemical and related process operatives
description: Execute or adapt procedures and techniques for calibrating measurement devices, by applying knowledge of measurement science, mathematics, physics, chemistry, and electronics, sometimes under the direction of engineering staff. Determine measurement standard suitability for calibrating measurement devices. May perform preventive maintenance on equipment. May perform corrective actions to address identified calibration problems. All engineering technologists and technicians, except drafters, not listed separately. Test the safety of structures, vehicles, or vessels using x-ray, ultrasound, fiber optic or related equipment. (Based on 76 related US occupations.)

work_activities.element_name: Communicating with Supervisors, Peers, or Subordinates
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
Operating Vehicles, Mechanized Devices, or Equipment
Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information
Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
Coordinating the Work and Activities of Others
Interpreting the Meaning of Information for Others
Communicating with People Outside the Organization
Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

work_context.element_name: Face-to-Face Discussions with Individuals and Within Teams
Work With or Contribute to a Work Group or Team
Deal With External Customers or the Public in General
Coordinate or Lead Others in Accomplishing Work Activities
Health and Safety of Other Workers
Work Outcomes and Results of Other Workers
Dealing With Unpleasant, Angry, or Discourteous People
Dealing with Violent or Physically Aggressive People
Outdoors, Exposed to All Weather Conditions
In an Open Vehicle or Operating Equipment
In an Enclosed Vehicle or Operate Enclosed Equipment
Exposed to Sounds, Noise Levels that are Distracting or Uncomfortable
Exposed to Very Hot or Cold Temperatures
Exposed to Extremely Bright or Inadequate Lighting Conditions
Exposed to Cramped Work Space, Awkward Positions
Exposed to Minor Burns, Cuts, Bites, or Stings
Spend Time Climbing Ladders, Scaffolds, or Poles
Spend Time Kneeling, Crouching, Stooping, or Crawling
Spend Time Keeping or Regaining Balance
Spend Time Using Your Hands to Handle, Control, or Feel Objects, Tools, or Controls
Spend Time Bending or Twisting Your Body
Wear Common Protective or Safety Equipment such as Safety Shoes, Glasses, Gloves, Hearing Protection, Hard Hats, or Life Jackets
Wear Specialized Protective or Safety Equipment such as Breathing Apparatus, Safety Harness, Full Protection Suits, or Radiation Protection
Impact of Decisions on Co-workers or Company Results
Importance of Being Exact or Accurate
Pace Determined by Speed of Equipment

tasks.task: Monitor transportation and storage of flammable and other potentially dangerous products to ensure that safety guidelines are followed.
Read and analyze specifications, schedules, logs, test results, and laboratory recommendations to determine how to set equipment controls to produce the required qualities and quantities of products.
Synchronize activities with other pumphouses to ensure a continuous flow of products and a minimum of contamination between products.
Patrol units to monitor the amount of oil in storage tanks, and to verify that activities and operations are safe, efficient, and in compliance with regulations.
Maintain and repair equipment, or report malfunctioning equipment to supervisors so that repairs can be scheduled.
Collect product samples by turning bleeder valves, or by lowering containers into tanks to obtain oil samples.
Inspect pipelines, tightening connections and lubricating valves as necessary.
Conduct general housekeeping of units, including wiping up oil spills and performing general cleaning duties.
Coordinate shutdowns and major projects.
Perform tests to check the qualities and grades of products, such as assessing levels of bottom sediment, water, and foreign materials in oil samples, using centrifugal testers.
Prepare calculations for receipts and deliveries of oil and oil products.
Lower thermometers into tanks to obtain temperature readings and make necessary adjustments to ensure proper performance.
Clean interiors of processing units by circulating chemicals and solvents within units.
Clamp seals around valves to secure tanks and ensure they are operational.
Calculate test result values, using standard formulas, and ensure accuracy in measurements.
Read gas meters and maintain records of the amounts of gas received and dispensed from holders.
Operate power-driven pumps that transfer liquids, semi-liquids, gases, or powdered materials.
Record instrument readings and operational changes in operating logs and follow established reporting protocols.
Move controls and turn valves to start compressor engines, pumps, and auxiliary equipment as needed.
Turn knobs or switches to regulate pressures and respond to operational needs.
Respond to problems by adjusting control room equipment or instructing other personnel to adjust equipment at problem locations.
Take samples of gases and conduct chemical tests to determine gas quality and sulfur or moisture content, or send samples to laboratories for analysis.
Clean, lubricate, and adjust equipment, and replace filters and gaskets, using hand tools.
Submit daily reports on facility operations and ensure compliance with all operational standards.
Connect pipelines between pumps and containers that are being filled or emptied to ensure efficient operation.
Maintain each station by performing general housekeeping duties such as painting, washing, and cleaning.
Record and compile operating data, instrument readings, documentation, and results of laboratory analyses.
Monitor meters and pressure gauges to determine consumption rate variations, temperatures, and pressures and ensure operational integrity.
Plan movement of products through lines to processing, storage, and shipping units, using knowledge of system interconnections and capacities.
Distribute or process gas for utility companies or industrial plants, using panel boards, control boards, and semi-automatic equipment.
Operate auxiliary equipment and control multiple processing units during distilling or treating operations, adjusting controls as necessary.
Monitor equipment functioning, observe temperature, level, and flow gauges, and perform regular unit checks to ensure that all equipment is operating as it should.
Control operation of compressors, scrubbers, evaporators, and refrigeration equipment to liquefy, compress, or regasify natural gas.
Start and shut down plant equipment in accordance with safety protocols.
Record, review, and compile operations records, test results, and gauge readings such as temperatures, pressures, concentrations, and flows.
Adjust temperature, pressure, vacuum, level, flow rate, or transfer of gas to maintain processes at required levels or to correct problems.
Clean, maintain, and repair equipment, using hand tools, or request that repair and maintenance work be performed as needed.
Determine causes of abnormal pressure variances and make corrective recommendations, such as installation of pipes to relieve overloading.
Read logsheets to determine product demand and disposition, or to detect malfunctions.
Test gas, chemicals, and air during processing to assess factors such as purity and moisture content, and to detect quality problems or gas or chemical leaks.
Contact maintenance crews when necessary to ensure a quick response to equipment issues.
Change charts in recording meters to ensure accurate data tracking.
Collaborate with other operators to solve unit problems and improve operational efficiency.
Control or operate manifold and pumping systems to circulate liquids through a petroleum refinery.
Control fractioning columns, compressors, purifying towers, heat exchangers, and related equipment to extract nitrogen and oxygen from air when necessary.
Calculate gas ratios to detect deviations from specifications, using testing apparatus for quality assurance.
Signal or direct workers who tend auxiliary equipment to ensure proper coordination of operations.
Operate construction equipment to install and maintain gas distribution systems as needed.
Monitor process indicators, instruments, gauges, and meters to detect and report any possible problems promptly.
Start pumps and open valves or use automated equipment to regulate the flow of oil in pipelines and into and out of tanks, ensuring compliance with safety and operational standards.
Operate control panels to coordinate and regulate process variables such as temperature and pressure, and to direct product flow rate, according to process schedules.
Signal other workers by telephone or radio to operate pumps, open and close valves, and check temperatures as directed.
Verify that incoming and outgoing products are moving through the correct meters, and that meters are working properly.
Control equipment to regulate flow and pressure of gas to feedlines of boilers, furnaces, and related steam-generating or heating equipment safely.
Read automatic gauges at specified intervals to determine the flow rate of oil into or from tanks, and the amount of oil in tanks as part of operational monitoring.
Monitor or operate boilers, turbines, wells, or auxiliary power plant equipment to ensure efficient and effective operation.
Record operating data, such as the results of surveillance tests for auditing and quality control purposes.
Dispatch orders or instructions to personnel through radiotelephone or intercommunication systems to coordinate auxiliary equipment operation effectively.
Confer with technical and supervisory personnel to report or resolve conditions affecting safety, efficiency, or product quality, ensuring operational integrity.
Conduct inspections or operations outside of control rooms as necessary to maintain safety standards.
Note malfunctions of equipment, instruments, or controls and report these conditions to supervisors for timely resolution.
Participate in nuclear fuel element handling activities, such as preparation, transfer, loading, or unloading, in accordance with safety procedures.
Draw samples of products and conduct quality control tests to monitor processing and ensure that standards are met consistently.
Direct reactor operators in emergency situations, in accordance with emergency operating procedures to ensure safety.
Authorize maintenance activities on units or changes in equipment or system operational status as necessary.
Interpret chemical reactions visible through sight glasses or on television monitors and review laboratory test reports for process adjustments.

Start pumps to wash and rinse reactor vessels, to exhaust gases or vapors, to regulate the flow of oil, steam, air, or perfume to towers, or to add products to converter or blending vessels as required.
Regulate or shut down equipment during emergency situations, as directed by supervisory personnel.
Monitor all systems for normal running conditions, performing activities such as checking gauges to assess output or the effects of generator loading on other equipment appropriately.
Implement operational procedures, such as those controlling start-up or shut-down activities, in alignment with safety protocols.

Inspect water-powered electric generators or auxiliary equipment in hydroelectric plants to verify proper operation or to determine maintenance or repair needs, ensuring compliance with safety regulations.
Adjust controls to position rod and to regulate flux level, reactor period, coolant temperature, or rate of power flow, following standard procedures.
Operate equipment to start, stop, or regulate biomass-fueled generators, generator units, boilers, engines, or auxiliary systems in compliance with operational guidelines.
Operate high-pressure steam boiler or water chiller equipment for electrical cogeneration operations safely and efficiently.
Preprocess feedstock to prepare for biochemical or thermochemical production processes as necessary to enhance productivity.
Measure and monitor raw biomass feedstock, including wood, waste, or refuse materials to ensure quality standards are met.
Operate valves, pumps, engines, or generators to control and adjust production of biofuels or biomass-fueled power effectively.
Perform routine maintenance or make minor repairs to mechanical, electrical, or electronic equipment in biomass plants to ensure operational readiness.
Assess quality of biomass feedstock and make adjustments as necessary to maintain process efficiency.
Calculate, measure, load, or mix biomass feedstock for power generation accurately to optimize production efficiency.
Calibrate liquid flow devices or meters, including fuel, chemical, and water meters to ensure accurate operation and compliance.
Inspect biomass power plant or processing equipment, recording or reporting damage and mechanical problems for maintenance action.
Operate biomass fuel-burning boiler or biomass fuel gasification system equipment in accordance with specifications or instructions.
Operate equipment to heat biomass, using knowledge of controls, combustion, and firing mechanisms to maximize efficiency.
Maintain logs, reports, work requests, or other records of work performed in hydroelectric plants for compliance and audits.
Respond to system or unit abnormalities, diagnosing the cause, and recommending or taking corrective action as needed to maintain safety standards.
Operate high voltage switches or related devices in hydropower stations safely and efficiently.
Record and compile operational data by completing and maintaining forms, logs, or reports for regulatory compliance.
Operate, control, or monitor integrated gasification combined cycle (IGCC) or related equipment, such as air separation units, to generate electricity from coal as needed.

Operate, control, or monitor gasifiers or related equipment, such as coolers, water quenches, water gas shifts reactors, or sulfur recovery units, to produce syngas or electricity from coal as necessary.
Operate, control, or monitor equipment, such as acid or gas carbon dioxide removal units, carbon dioxide compressors, or pipelines, to capture, store, or transport carbon dioxide exhaust effectively.
Operate or maintain distributed power generation equipment, including fuel cells or microturbines, to produce energy on-site for manufacturing or other commercial purposes appropriately.
Supervise technicians' work activities to ensure that equipment is operated in accordance with policies and procedures that protect workers from radiation and ensure environmental safety.
Operate nuclear power reactors in accordance with policies and procedures to protect workers from radiation and to ensure environmental safety rigorously.
Direct the collection and testing of air, water, gas, or solid samples to determine radioactivity levels or to ensure appropriate radioactive containment as necessary.
Direct measurement of the intensity or types of radiation in work areas, equipment, or materials carefully to ensure compliance with safety standards.
Authorize actions to correct identified operational inefficiencies or hazards so that operating efficiency is maximized and potential environmental issues are minimized as needed.
Communicate with systems operators to regulate and coordinate line voltages and transmission loads and frequencies effectively.
Open and close valves and switches in sequence to start or shut down auxiliary units systematically to maintain operational flow.

Control or maintain auxiliary equipment, such as pumps, fans, compressors, condensers, feedwater heaters, filters, or chlorinators, to supply water, fuel, lubricants, air, or auxiliary power routinely.
Make adjustments or minor repairs, such as tightening leaking gland or pipe joints, proactively to avoid larger issues.
Control generator output to match the phase, frequency, or voltage of electricity supplied to panels accurately.
Place standby emergency electrical generators on line in emergencies and monitor the temperature, output, and lubrication of the system to ensure reliability.
Analyze the layout, instrumentation, or function of electrical generation or transmission facilities as necessary.
Diagnose or troubleshoot problems with gas collection systems proactively as needed.
Monitor well fields periodically to ensure proper functioning and performance.
Operate landfill gas, methane, or natural gas fueled electrical generation systems efficiently.
Prepare and submit compliance, operational, and safety forms or reports accurately and timely.
Repair or replace gas piping as necessary to maintain structural integrity.
Clean, lubricate, or maintain equipment, such as generators, turbines, pumps, or compressors, to prevent failure or deterioration.
Operate hydroelectric plant equipment, such as turbines, pumps, valves, gates, fans, electric control boards, or battery banks effectively to ensure optimal operation.
Verify that well field monitoring data conforms to applicable regulations as needed.
Manipulate controls to adjust or activate power distribution equipment or machines as needed.

Control, monitor, or operate equipment and machinery that regulates or distributes electricity, steam, and other substances, using data obtained from instruments or computers, including starting or stopping generators, turbines, or other power plant equipment as necessary.

Coordinate with engineers, planners, and other personnel to provide information and implement actions for the safe operation of electrical and distribution equipment, such as lockouts, tagouts, and following switching orders while ensuring compliance with relevant standards.

Monitor and record operational data and equipment conditions, including inspecting equipment for defects, assessing equipment operating status, and reading switchboard or control board readings to ensure proper functioning.

Test and calibrate measurement devices and materials, ensuring compliance with specifications by utilizing knowledge of measurement science, and performing necessary preventive maintenance or corrective actions as required.
Perform equipment and machinery maintenance as needed, including repairing or replacing damaged equipment, lubricating components, and cleaning vessels or pumps using appropriate tools and materials.

Plan the movement of products through processing and storage, determining equipment requirements and load estimates, while ensuring effective operation of distribution systems under anticipated conditions.
Tend auxiliary equipment related to power distribution and treatment processes, making necessary adjustments to maintain specifications and operational efficiency.
Inspect and clean machinery and equipment to ensure compliance with safety standards and operational specifications, including testing machinery with suitable devices as needed.
Record and compile data related to operational conditions and equipment performance, analyzing this information to inform adjustments and improvements.
Evaluate logs and materials for quality, characteristics, and compliance with specifications, and classify them accordingly based on established criteria.
Identify equipment needs and manage necessary materials based on job requirements and environmental factors, including estimating costs and measuring materials accurately based on specifications.
Conduct various tests and inspections of electrical and chemical processes using specialized equipment to ensure operational standards are maintained effectively.
Monitor gauges and flowmeters, inspect equipment to ensure that tank levels, temperatures, chemical amounts, and pressures are at specified levels, and report abnormalities as necessary.

Connect hoses and pipelines to pumps and vessels prior to material transfer using hand tools, and add dyes, water, detergents, or chemicals to tanks to dilute or strengthen solutions according to established formulas and solution test results.

Measure and mix chemicals to prepare solutions for processing according to formulas, and fill tanks of processing machines with solutions such as developer, dyes, stop-bath, fixers, bleaches, or washes.
Maintain records of quantities or types of processing completed, materials used, or customer charges, and record production data such as numbers and types of bobbins wound.

Check equipment to ensure proper operation, monitor equipment operations to detect malfunctions, and notify supervisors or mechanics of equipment malfunctions, and perform maintenance such as cleaning and oiling machines.

Move controls to activate and adjust machines and equipment to regulate pressure, temperature, and speed, and observe display screens, control panels, equipment, and product processes to determine if machines are operating correctly and make adjustments as necessary.

Observe operations to detect defects or supply shortages and examine products for surface and dimension defects using precision measuring instruments, grading and sorting products according to quality to ensure correct processing and usage.
Sew, join, reinforce, or finish parts of articles using needles and thread or other materials, and trim excess threads or edges of parts using scissors or knives.

Start machines, monitor operation, and make adjustments as needed to ensure specifications are met during production runs, and stop machines when specified amounts of products have been produced or when malfunctions are detected.
Examine developed prints for defects and create prints according to customer specifications and laboratory protocols, while reviewing computer-processed digital images for quality.
Perform machine setup by reading specifications, blueprints, and work orders to determine setups, temperatures, and time settings required for processing materials.

Remove inferior or defective products and discard foreign matter, placing acceptable products in containers for further processing, and notify other workers of defects and direct adjustments to machinery as necessary.
Inspect machinery to determine necessary adjustments and repairs, and examine equipment to ensure it is operating properly.
Load materials into machines using hand tools, and observe continuous operation to ensure that production meets specifications before adjusting settings as necessary.
Perform preventive maintenance on equipment and execute corrective actions to address identified problems or malfunctions during operations.
Provide assistance to survey crews and perform supplemental tasks under guidance, including maintenance of safety standards and environmental protocols in operational settings.
Inspect products to verify that they meet specifications and determine whether machine adjustment is needed.
Replace depleted supply packages with full packages.
Flush or clean equipment using steam hoses or mechanical reamers, and drain equipment to pump water or other solutions through to flush and clean tanks or equipment.
Make minor repairs, lubricate, and maintain equipment using hand tools.
Observe and compare colors and consistencies of products to instrument readings and standard test results.
Implement appropriate industrial emergency response procedures and observe safety precautions to prevent fires or explosions.
Estimate materials required for production and manufacturing of products, and read plant specifications to determine products, ingredients, or prescribed modifications of procedures.
Open valves or start pumps, agitators, reactors, blowers, or automatic feed of materials.
Inspect equipment or units to detect leaks or malfunctions, shutting equipment down if necessary, and notify maintenance engineers of malfunctions.
Record operational data such as temperatures, pressures, ingredients used, processing times, or test results.
Draw samples of products at specified stages for analysis, and test product samples for specific gravity, chemical characteristics, pH levels, concentrations, or viscosities.
Patrol work areas to detect leaks or equipment malfunctions and monitor operating conditions.
Measure, weigh, and mix chemical ingredients according to specifications.
Control or operate equipment in which chemical changes or reactions take place during processing of products.
Direct activities of workers assisting in control or verification of processes or in unloading materials.
Press buttons, pull levers, or depress pedals to start and operate cutting and slicing machines, and monitor operation to detect malfunctions.
Examine, measure, and weigh materials or products to verify conformity to specifications using measuring devices.
Set up, operate, or tend machines that cut or slice materials, and stack and sort cut material for packaging or further processing.
Review work orders, blueprints, specifications, or job samples to ascertain components, settings, and adjustments for machines.
Remove defective or substandard materials from machines and readjust machine components for standards compliance.
Adjust machine controls to alter position, alignment, speed, or pressure.
Maintain production records including quantities, types, and dimensions of materials produced.
Start machines to verify setups and make necessary adjustments.
Monitor gauges, recording instruments, flowmeters, or products to ensure specified conditions are maintained.
Adjust controls to regulate temperature, pressure, feed, or flow of liquids or gases during processing.
Test and analyze samples to determine content and characteristics using laboratory apparatus or testing equipment.
Clean and maintain work areas and equipment, using hand tools.
Transport materials and products to and from work areas using carts, handtrucks, or hoists.
Operate and maintain equipment, perform minor repairs, and clean equipment as needed.
Measure, mix, and test solutions or chemicals for processing and ensure conformance to standards using various measuring devices.
Monitor machine operations and environmental conditions, observe gauges and controls to ensure optimal processing conditions, and make necessary adjustments.
Record and maintain production data, such as materials processed, operational logs, and time reports.
Examine, measure, and evaluate materials or products to ensure they meet established specifications and quality control measures.
Load, unload, and transfer materials between containers and work areas, ensuring efficient processing.
Communicate processing instructions and work with other team members to meet production goals and standards.
Adjust controls and dials on machinery to regulate processing functions such as flow rates, temperatures, and pressures.
Inspect equipment for hazards, operating efficiency, and perform maintenance or repairs as necessary.
Activate, monitor, and troubleshoot automated equipment during production runs to ensure proper operation and quality output.
Perform routine maintenance on mechanical, electrical, or electronic equipment or instruments used in the processing of biofuels and calibrate them to required tolerances.
Monitor and adjust operations of geothermal power plant equipment or systems, and inspect biofuels processing equipment regularly to record or report damage and mechanical problems.
Collect and record data associated with operating geothermal power plants or well fields and monitor stored biofuels products until reused or transferred to users.
Clean work areas and biofuels processing work area, ensuring compliance with safety regulations.
Identify and correct malfunctions of geothermal plant equipment, electrical systems, and instruments, and troubleshoot systems to determine required repairs.
Inspect, test, or measure materials, products, or installations for conformance to specifications, including performing pre- and post-installation tests of geothermal loop piping.
Read work orders, blueprints, data, and other materials to determine production specifications and procedures required for installation, maintenance, or repair.
Prepare and maintain logs, reports, or other documentation of work performed, and write inspection reports to describe results or needed repairs.
Remove defects, such as chips or burrs from equipment, and clean, maintain, calibrate, or repair measuring instruments or test equipment.
Diagnose faults or malfunctions to determine required repairs and conduct preventative maintenance to ensure operational efficiency.
Analyze test data and make computations as necessary to determine test results, and test samples of materials or products for compliance with specifications.
Operate equipment and conduct necessary testing, including calibrated devices and flow meters, to control processes in biofuels production.
Conduct routine inspections of equipment and monitor performance of biofuels processing data for quality assurance.
Collect samples of materials or products for laboratory testing and assess the quality of biofuels additives.
Inspect and repair defective components in manufacturing or biosystems as needed, including biofuels processing systems and geothermal systems.
Test mechanical products and equipment after repair or assembly to ensure compliance with manufacturers' specifications.
Operate valves, pumps, engines, or generators to control and adjust production processes, ensuring conformance to specifications.
Perform corrective actions to address identified calibration problems by making adjustments or recommending necessary repairs based on inspection results.
Monitor biofuels production operations, maintain inventories of tools and parts, and coordinate sourcing of raw materials or products.
Understand and apply measurement science and techniques for calibration of instruments relevant to the chemical process industry.

Lay out mounting holes using measuring instruments, drill holes with a power drill, and assemble machines by bolting, welding, riveting, or otherwise fastening them to foundations or other structures using hand tools and power tools.

Clean machines and machine parts using cleaning solvents, cloths, air guns, hoses, vacuums, or other equipment, and lubricate or apply adhesives or other materials to machines, machine parts, or other equipment according to specified procedures.

Observe and test the operation of machinery or equipment to diagnose malfunctions using voltmeters or other testing devices, and analyze test results, machine error messages, or information obtained from operators to diagnose equipment problems.

Operate newly repaired machinery or equipment to verify the adequacy of repairs and reassemble machines after the completion of repair or maintenance work, including testing operations and making adjustments as necessary.

Record repairs and maintenance performed, including details of repairs made and parts used, and inventory and requisition machine parts, equipment, and other supplies so that stock can be maintained and replenished.

Study blueprints or manufacturers' manuals to determine the correct installation or operation of machinery, read work orders and specifications to determine machines and equipment requiring repair or maintenance, and assess articles for defects, marking defective areas or advising supervisors of repair needs.
Dismantle defective machines for repair using hand tools and other equipment, and disassemble machinery or equipment to remove parts and make repairs, ensuring proper reassembly afterward.
Repair or replace broken or malfunctioning components of machinery or equipment, including repairing defective parts using hand tools, milling machines, lathes, welding equipment, grinders, or saws.
Fabricate new metal parts using drill presses, engine lathes, and other machine tools, and cut and weld metal to repair broken parts or assemble new equipment.
Test and replace electrical components and wiring using test meters, soldering equipment, and hand tools, and start machines to observe mechanical operation to ensure efficiency and detect problems.

Remove parts from furnaces after specified times, and air dry or cool parts in water, oil brine, or other baths, and adjust controls to maintain temperatures and heating times using thermal instruments and charts.

Inspect or test damaged machine parts and examine parts for defects, such as breakage or excessive wear, using various inspection tools including calipers and gauges to verify conformance to specifications.
Maintain, repair, and overhaul machinery and industrial production or processing equipment, including cleaning and lubricating parts, machinery, or equipment as necessary.
Transport machine parts, tools, equipment, and other material between work areas and storage using cranes, hoists, or dolly systems as required.
Collaborate with other workers to assist in repairing or moving machines, machine parts, or equipment, and instruct new workers on machine operation.
Record manufacturing information such as quantities, sizes, or types of goods produced, and verify accuracy of shapes and sizes of objects using calipers and templates.
Verify dimensions of products, such as cuts or carvings, using measuring instruments to ensure adherence to specifications, blueprints, or models.
Study blueprints, layouts, or work orders to determine specifications, sequences of operations, tooling instructions, and material requirements for machining.
Operate and tend machines, including pressing, forging, or casting equipment, to perform operations such as shaping, bending, or forming materials according to specifications.
Measure and mix materials or chemicals to specified formulations or consistencies, ensuring process adherence based on scientific principles.
Perform maintenance and repair on equipment, tools, and machines, including changing worn parts and ensuring proper functioning to prevent breakdowns.
Operate and adjust machine controls to ensure that processes meet specifications, including setting cutting speeds, feed rates, and tool positions.
Examine finished products for defects and ensure compliance with specifications, sorting defective pieces accordingly by type of flaw.
Select and install tooling and fixtures according to specifications, using appropriate tools for adjustments and installations.
Clean and lubricate work areas and machines to maintain safe and efficient working conditions.
Record production output, including operational data, and monitor machine cycles to ensure that products conform to specifications.
Turn valves or pull levers to start and regulate the flow of coolant or lubricant to work areas, fill oil cups, adjust valves, and observe gauges to control coolant and lubricant flow on workpieces.
Set up, operate, or tend machines for various operations such as sawing, cutting, and shaping materials; adjust machine controls to optimize performance and ensure product conformity.
Examine, inspect, and measure raw materials and finished products using tools such as gauges, micrometers, and calipers to verify conformance to specifications; document and report results.
Clean work areas and maintain a safe and orderly work environment, ensuring adherence to safety and sanitation regulations.
Install, maintain, and perform minor repairs on equipment; troubleshoot machinery and make adjustments to reduce defects and improve performance.
Direct and train other workers in processing tasks, operations, and safety procedures while maintaining operational efficiency and productivity.
Monitor inventory levels and requisition materials, tools, or equipment parts as needed to ensure continuous operations.
Calibrate measurement devices by comparing measurements to known standards; conduct tests to determine performance and reliability of equipment.
Conduct non-destructive tests to identify defects in materials or structures using techniques such as ultrasonic or thermal testing; interpret results and prepare reports.
Evaluate employee performance and conduct training to enhance skills related to equipment operations and safety procedures.
Plan and establish work schedules and assignments based on production goals and workforce availability; confer with supervisors and management to resolve any operational issues.
Maintain records of production, quality control, and safety inspections; compile operational statistics for management review.
Inspect and monitor tools and equipment to ensure they are in proper working order and meet safety standards.
Perform skilled repair and maintenance operations utilizing various hand and power tools or equipment as required.
Assist engineers in the development of new products, processes, and operational improvements.
Document calibration processes and results according to established procedures; maintain documentation for compliance and tracking.
